name: The Void Gates
subtitle: The Emperor watches from every shadow
theme: Doubt / Strength / The nature of darkness
location: Shadow Reach to the Fortress Outer Gate

shard.name: Seal Fragment of Wind
shard.description: A swirling green-gold crystal that moves like trapped breath. It whispers â€” not words, just the impression of them.

scenes.narration: The Shadow Reach is where Valdris's power is absolute â€” perpetual half-dark, where even shadows cast shadows. The air tastes like the moment before a storm that never arrives.
A voice fills the air â€” not through sound, but through the space between thoughts. No echo. No direction. Everywhere at once.

dialogue.speaker: narrator
dialogue.text: Valdris: Four Seal Fragments reclaimed. I confess I did not expect you to reach this gate intact. The Oracle made a better choice than I gave her credit for.

dialogue.speaker: Xiao
dialogue.text: You speak to distract. It will not work.

dialogue.speaker: narrator
dialogue.text: Valdris: No. I speak because I am curious. A yaksha who has carried karmic contamination for two thousand years â€” every demon you devoured left a scar, and you kept going. That is either extraordinary will or extraordinary self-destruction. I have not decided which.

dialogue.speaker: narrator
dialogue.text: Valdris: The funeral director. You have been around death long enough to know it does not frighten you â€” but you have never truly faced the possibility that all that poetry and laughter might simply... stop. Have you?

dialogue.speaker: Hutao
dialogue.text: I provide a service to those experiencing a natural transition. There is a difference between my death and the ones I manage.

dialogue.speaker: narrator
dialogue.text: Valdris: Is there? You are very convincing. You almost convinced yourself.

dialogue.speaker: narrator
dialogue.text: Valdris: The dancer with the water that answers her. The princess who carries the weight of a name larger than herself. I know all four of you better than you know each other. I have been patient. I have had time.

dialogue.speaker: Nilou
dialogue.text: You spoke to me in the water. You tried to make hopelessness feel reasonable. It did not work.

dialogue.speaker: narrator
dialogue.text: Valdris: No. But it planted something. It always does. You will feel it in the gate â€” when the Fallen Angel comes at you and you wonder, just for a moment, if breaking beautiful things is the only honest act left in this world.

dialogue.speaker: Ayaka
dialogue.text: Then we will answer that question by not breaking. Move. Together.

scenes.narration: The voice pulls back â€” not gone, just watching from a greater distance. Nilou lets out a slow, controlled breath.

dialogue.speaker: Nilou
dialogue.text: He knew things. Specific things. Not just who we are â€” how we think.

dialogue.speaker: Xiao
dialogue.text: I have carried karma for two thousand years. I have been afraid before. I fought anyway. That is the only answer that matters.

dialogue.speaker: Hutao
dialogue.text: ...Knowing someone and stopping them are very different problems. He knows us. We are about to stop him. Advantage: ours.

dialogue.speaker: Ayaka
dialogue.text: Well said. Both of you.

pre_dialogue.speaker: narrator
pre_dialogue.text: The Fallen Angel guards the fortress gate â€” a celestial warrior torn from divinity and rebuilt in Valdris's image. Vast wings of compressed shadow. Eyes with no memory of what they once were.

pre_dialogue.speaker: Xiao
pre_dialogue.text: She was something sacred once. The shadow takes what is strong and breaks it into tools.

pre_dialogue.speaker: Ayaka
pre_dialogue.text: Then we undo what he made of her. One way or another.

post_dialogue.speaker: narrator
post_dialogue.text: The Fallen Angel's shadow-form cracks. Light pours through the fractures â€” old light, from before the corruption. The Seal Fragment of Wind rises free, bright and clean.

post_dialogue.speaker: Nilou
post_dialogue.text: Every time we free one of these... it feels like something unclenching. Like a held breath, finally released.

post_dialogue.speaker: Hutao
post_dialogue.text: Four Seal Fragments. Four fights. Now we face the Emperor directly. I would be lying if I said I was not a little nervous.

post_dialogue.speaker: Xiao
post_dialogue.text: That is good. Nervousness keeps you precise.

post_dialogue.speaker: Ayaka
post_dialogue.text: Together. That is how we have done everything else. That is how we will finish this.

scenes.narration: The Wind Fragment settles into their hands. For a moment the fortress is still â€” the kind of still that comes before a sentence is finished.

dialogue.speaker: narrator
dialogue.text: Valdris: I made her.

dialogue.speaker: narrator
dialogue.text: Valdris: Centuries. Every flaw of her original form â€” corrected. Every limitation of divinity â€” stripped away. She was perfect. And you dismantled her in minutes.

dialogue.speaker: Hutao
dialogue.text: She was not yours to make.

dialogue.speaker: narrator
dialogue.text: Valdris: Everything in this world is mine. I built this silence over six hundred years. Bone by bone. World by world. You are four visitors with borrowed time, chipping at the edges of something you cannot comprehend.

dialogue.speaker: Ayaka
dialogue.text: We do not need to comprehend it. We just need to end it.

dialogue.speaker: narrator
dialogue.text: Valdris: Then come through that gate. Into the heart of everything I have become. And we will not ask who is stronger. We will ask â€” who is willing to pay the full cost of being right?

dialogue.speaker: narrator
dialogue.text: Valdris: I already paid mine. Centuries ago. The only question is whether you are ready to pay yours.

scenes.narration: The four stand before the last door â€” a gate of pure compressed shadow, the boundary between the fortress and the Emperor's inner sanctum.

dialogue.speaker: Hutao
dialogue.text: I keep thinking about the people back in Liyue. The contracts I left on my desk.

dialogue.speaker: Nilou
dialogue.text: I think about the Grand Bazaar. The crowd. The music starting. Whether they are wondering where I went.

dialogue.speaker: Ayaka
dialogue.text: My brother. He will be searching. He always knows when something has gone wrong.

dialogue.speaker: Xiao
dialogue.text: I think about whether I will see Liyue again. The lanterns at Lantern Rite. I did not know I valued those things until the possibility of losing them became real.

scenes.narration: A beat of shared silence. These were strangers two weeks ago.

dialogue.speaker: Hutao
dialogue.text: Well! Now that we have all gotten properly sentimental â€” shall we go end an Emperor?

dialogue.speaker: Nilou
dialogue.text: Let us.

dialogue.speaker: Ayaka
dialogue.text: For Aethoria. For our own worlds. Let us not fail.

dialogue.speaker: Xiao
dialogue.text: ...Let us not.

pre_dialogue.speaker: narrator
pre_dialogue.text: The Fallen Angel has been broken. The fortress gate lies open. And beyond it — the sanctum. Where Valdris watches. Waits.

pre_dialogue.speaker: Xiao
pre_dialogue.text: Four Fragments reclaimed. One remains. After this, there will be no turning back.

pre_dialogue.speaker: Hutao
pre_dialogue.text: Good. I am tired of running. Tired of doubting. Let us end this.

pre_dialogue.speaker: Ayaka
pre_dialogue.text: Together. As we have done this entire time. That is what he cannot break.

post_dialogue.speaker: narrator
post_dialogue.text: But when the shadow falls back, something is different. Valdris does not collapse. Valdris does not break. Valdris simply... steps aside.

post_dialogue.speaker: narrator
post_dialogue.text: Valdris: Well done. The Emperor has waited long enough in the wings. Perhaps it is time for an introduction. Or a reckoning. The distinction matters less than you might think.

post_dialogue.speaker: Ayaka
post_dialogue.text: One more. One more Fragment, and then we face him directly. We are close.
